label: runtime contracts plus outcome evaluator
summary: A goal-backed strategy artifact that first diagnoses why growth is blocked, then selects measurable experiments with clear owner split and stop conditions.

root_causes: TagBack has not separated acquisition, activation, and retention causes, so marketing activity can hide product-positioning failure.
The app value proposition is likely too broad unless a specific recall/reminder job is named.
No measurement loop proves whether the bottleneck is impression-to-install, install-to-first-tag, or first-tag-to-repeat use.
why_now: Before buying traffic or posting broadly, TagBack needs one narrow wedge where conversion can be measured within a week.
rejected_hypotheses: More channels by itself is rejected because it cannot explain which funnel step improves.
A full rebrand is rejected until a segment-specific message test fails.

primary_segment: People who repeatedly save links, photos, or notes and fail to rediscover them at the moment of need.
job_to_be_done: When I capture something useful, help me tag and resurface it later without building a manual organization system.
activation_trigger: First successful saved item that is later found again through tag recall.

positioning: Position TagBack as a recall utility, not a generic notes app.
channels: Short-form demos showing lost item -> tag -> rediscovery.
App Store search terms around tag, bookmark, recall, organize, and remember.
Small creator or student workflow communities where repeated capture pain is visible.
message_tests: Find what you saved without folders.
Tag once, get it back when you need it.

ranked_experiments: E1: landing/App Store message test around recall utility before channel expansion.
E2: onboarding event instrumentation from install to first meaningful tag.
E3: 10-user concierge interviews to identify the strongest repeat-use segment.
selection_reason: E1 and E2 isolate whether demand or activation is the binding constraint; E3 explains the why behind numbers.
kill_or_continue_rule: Kill a message if click-to-install or install-to-first-tag does not improve after 100 qualified impressions or one week.
Continue only when the metric movement identifies the next bottleneck.

north_star_metric: Weekly users who retrieve a previously tagged item.

codex_owned_actions: Audit current App Store metadata and onboarding text.
Create an event instrumentation checklist and acceptance criteria.
Produce a one-week experiment board with score columns.
user_owned_actions: Provide current analytics screenshots or confirm none exist.
Approve which segment is ethically and commercially worth targeting.
Publish tests and collect real traffic data.

risks: Synthetic strategy may overfit assumptions without real analytics.
A niche wedge may be too narrow if retention is already healthy.
Marketing copy can promise retrieval behavior the product does not yet support.
guardrails: Separate known, inferred, and unknown evidence before claiming product-market fit.
Do not spend on paid acquisition until activation tracking works.
Return to Uigwe if metrics contradict the selected segment or success criteria.

known: The user asked for growth success, marketing reasons, causes, and plan quality.
inferred: TagBack likely depends on repeat retrieval, so retention metrics matter more than raw installs.
unknown: Current acquisition sources, App Store conversion, active users, retention, pricing, and onboarding drop-off are unknown.

first_week_plan: Day 2: choose one target segment and create three message variants.
Day 3: instrument or manually track the first-tag and first-retrieval funnel.
Days 4-6: run the highest-confidence channel/message test.
Day 7: compare metric movement and decide continue, kill, or re-enter planning.
dependencies: Current app screenshots, App Store listing, available analytics, and one reachable user segment.
verification_steps: Run the same rubric after the experiment artifact is produced.
Check whether every recommendation maps to a measurable funnel event.
Reject the plan if it cannot name the next bottleneck after one week.
